mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) captures real output momentum; sustained expansion signals higher corporate earnings capacity and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO surprises do not outperform zero-signal baseline with t-test p < 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5%, further realized increases in the rate trigger restrictive financial conditions that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising, or Rank IC turns positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) jointly signal a contraction in aggregate demand and income that amplifies recession risk beyond the effect of either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term does not improve predictive R-squared over single-series models by at least 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below its 12-month moving average, a subsequent rise in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points within a single month signals an inflection in labor market tightness that reduces consumption expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month while below 12-month average
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances following the trigger, or Sharpe of the signal is indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect forward-looking residential investment demand and construction sector health, which spills over into broader equity earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive HOUST surprises do not exceed the unconditional base rate by 2 percentage points annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3.0%, a further acceleration in the monthly CPI level signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain higher real rates for longer, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and monthly level accelerates
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or the return conditional on this signal is not significantly lower than the unconditional return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the prior 3 months and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) identifies a stagflationary tightening dynamic where monetary restriction damages labor demand; this joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that is stronger than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction portfolio fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of joint-signal months, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 5-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In quarters when real GDP (GDPC1) growth is positive but decelerating, an unexpected drop in industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month confirms a supply-side drag on the expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates quarter-over-quarter and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's information coefficient is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
